## What was decided / what is true

- Q3 priorities ordered: BADAS production stability first, Atlas customer onboarding second, Acko launch contingency third, Nuro pilot delivery fourth.
- BADAS 2.0 launch on April 16 confirmed clean. Naftali assumes API and UI ownership going forward; the recurring launch sync is wound down.
- Atlas workspaces functional and now the daily iteration tool for clients. Waymo is scaling its usage and the expansion conversation is active.
- Acko Pico launch BIS NOC certification still blocked. Mid-to-late May is the realistic re-window. ODM production slowdown contingent. Plan B design assigned to platform team this week.
- Nuro SOW signed; pilot kicking off; 1,000 clips at $150 per clip; expansion option up to 9,000 additional at $80.50 per clip. First delivery one month from signature.
- IBM workzones partnership demo Tuesday; roles divided cleanly between Nexar pipeline+APIs and IBM UI+permits.

## Why

- BADAS production handoff frees the launch team for next-iteration work; April performance justifies the move.
- Acko delay is the carry-over Q2 risk; ODM slowdown is the lever forcing the Plan B work this week.
- Nuro first-delivery commitment is contractual; no slip room.

## Load-bearing constraints

- BADAS edge latency under 250ms still unmet — gating factor for connected-camera rollout.
- Acko BIS NOC re-window: mid-to-late May or ODM slowdown triggers.
- Nuro first-delivery: signature-plus-thirty-days.
- IBM workzones roles: Nexar pipeline+APIs, IBM UI+permits.

## What to remember when this comes up later

- Q3 priority order is locked: BADAS, Atlas, Acko, Nuro — in that order.
- Atlas Waymo scaling discussion is active but not decided; self-serve and offshore-service options are on the table.
- Naftali owns BADAS API+UI; route operational issues to him going forward.
